title: Metric Taxonomy for Valuation Driver Analysis
note: Streamlined from prior run (25 metrics) by removing management fee revenue and its YoY growth from fee_quality — these are intermediate inputs to management fee rate, not independent drivers.

metrics.name: Distributable Earnings per Share
metrics.calculation_notes: Distributable earnings / diluted share count. DE is a non-GAAP measure with significant definition heterogeneity across firms — some include realized performance fees, others exclude them. Some firms deduct equity-based compensation, others do not. Tax treatment varies (pre-tax vs. after-tax DE). Always record each firm's specific DE definition alongside the value.

metrics.name: Earnings per Share (GAAP)
metrics.calculation_notes: GAAP net income attributable to common shareholders / diluted share count. For firms reporting under IFRS, use IFRS diluted EPS. GAAP EPS for alt managers is highly volatile due to mark-to-market on carried interest and unrealized investment income — expected to be a noisier signal than DE/share.

metrics.name: DE/Share Growth, 3-Year CAGR
metrics.calculation_notes: (DE_share_t / DE_share_t-3)^(1/3) - 1. Requires at least 4 annual data points. Undefined when base-year DE is negative or zero — record as N/A. M&A activity can distort growth rates if share count changed materially.

metrics.name: Fee-Earning AUM
metrics.calculation_notes: Subset of total AUM on which the firm earns management fees. Definitions vary: some firms count committed but uninvested capital, others only invested capital. Equivalent terms include 'Fee-Paying AUM' and 'Fee-Generating AUM.' Convert to USD at period-end exchange rates for non-USD denominated funds.

metrics.name: Total AUM
metrics.calculation_notes: Total assets under management including both fee-earning and non-fee-earning assets (e.g., dry powder, co-investment capital). Exclude 'Assets Under Advisement' if separately disclosed. Less standardized than FEAUM — double-counting risk with fund-of-funds or leveraged vehicles.

metrics.name: FEAUM Growth, Year-over-Year
metrics.calculation_notes: (FEAUM_t / FEAUM_t-1) - 1. Use end-of-fiscal-year values. Captures both organic and M&A-driven AUM growth — less clean as a signal than organic growth. FX movements affect USD-denominated FEAUM for non-USD fund managers.

metrics.name: FEAUM Growth, 3-Year CAGR
metrics.calculation_notes: (FEAUM_t / FEAUM_t-3)^(1/3) - 1. Smooths year-to-year volatility; provides a medium-term growth trajectory. Requires 4 data points. Includes inorganic growth; firms with recent IPOs or major restructuring may have non-comparable base-year figures.

metrics.name: Net Organic FEAUM Growth
metrics.calculation_notes: (Gross inflows - outflows - realizations) / beginning-of-period FEAUM. Excludes M&A-driven AUM additions and market appreciation/depreciation. The cleanest measure of franchise strength. Not all firms provide a clean organic growth figure — may require decomposing the AUM bridge into organic inflows, market effects, realizations, and M&A.

metrics.name: Fundraising as Percentage of AUM
metrics.calculation_notes: Total capital raised in period / beginning-of-period AUM * 100. Includes new fund closes, continuation vehicles, and incremental commitments to existing open-end vehicles; excludes reinvested distributions. Fundraising is lumpy — large flagship fund closes create spikes.

metrics.name: Asset Class Herfindahl-Hirschman Index
metrics.calculation_notes: Sum(share_i^2) * 10,000, where share_i is the fraction of total AUM in asset class i. Higher HHI = more concentrated (10,000 = single asset class). Lower HHI signals more diversified fee streams. Standardize across firms using 5-7 asset class categories: private equity, credit, real assets/infrastructure, hedge fund solutions, insurance solutions, other.

metrics.name: Permanent Capital Percentage
metrics.calculation_notes: Perpetual/open-end/evergreen AUM / total AUM * 100. Permanent capital includes publicly listed vehicles (BDCs, REITs), perpetual private funds, insurance general accounts, and long-duration vehicles with no fixed end date. Higher permanent capital implies more predictable management fee streams with lower re-fundraising risk.

metrics.name: Credit Percentage of AUM
metrics.calculation_notes: Credit AUM / total AUM * 100. Credit strategies include direct lending, distressed debt, structured credit (CLOs), mezzanine, and investment-grade private credit. Excludes equity-oriented special situations. Credit is the fastest-growing segment in alternatives — high exposure may signal growth optionality.

metrics.name: Fee-Related Earnings Margin
metrics.calculation_notes: FRE / management fee revenue * 100. Measures operating leverage on the fee-earning business, excluding performance income. FRE is non-GAAP with significant definition differences: (1) whether equity-based compensation is deducted, (2) whether transaction/advisory fees are included in revenue, (3) treatment of distribution/placement costs. Document each firm's definition.

metrics.name: FRE Growth, Year-over-Year
metrics.calculation_notes: (FRE_t / FRE_t-1) - 1. Captures both revenue growth and margin expansion/contraction. Undefined when prior-year FRE is negative. M&A-driven revenue additions and one-time items (e.g., catch-up fees) can distort.

metrics.name: Compensation-to-Revenue Ratio
metrics.calculation_notes: Total compensation and benefits expense / total revenue * 100. Lower ratios indicate greater operating leverage. Revenue denominator is volatile due to performance fees — computing on fee revenue only may yield a cleaner signal. Partnership structures may obscure true compensation costs.

metrics.name: Management Fee Rate
metrics.calculation_notes: Management fee revenue / average FEAUM * 10,000 (expressed as FRE/FEAUM in basis points). Use average FEAUM (beginning + end of period / 2) to avoid timing distortions. Higher rates indicate pricing power and/or mix tilted toward higher-fee strategies. Blended rate masks strategy-level variation.

metrics.name: Performance Fee Share of Total Revenue
metrics.calculation_notes: (Performance/incentive fees + realized carried interest) / total revenue * 100. Measures revenue cyclicality and upside optionality. Highly volatile year-to-year depending on vintage harvesting cycles. Market may discount high performance fee share (preferring stable fees) or reward it (as optionality) — direction of correlation is theoretically ambiguous.

metrics.name: Price-to-Fee-Related Earnings
metrics.calculation_notes: Market capitalization / annual FRE. The most widely used valuation multiple for listed alt managers. Market cap = share price * diluted shares (treasury stock method). Use consistent reference date for share price across the sample. Undefined when FRE is negative. For multi-class share structures, use total economic market cap.

metrics.name: Price-to-Distributable Earnings
metrics.calculation_notes: Market capitalization / annual DE. Broader than P/FRE — captures full economic earnings including realized performance income. DE includes realized performance fees which are volatile, making P/DE less stable than P/FRE across periods. Undefined when DE is negative.

metrics.name: Enterprise Value-to-Fee-Earning AUM
metrics.calculation_notes: (Market cap + total debt - cash) / FEAUM * 100. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 5% = $0.05 of EV per $1 of FEAUM). Use only corporate-level debt; exclude fund-level non-recourse debt. Minority interests in fund management subsidiaries may require adjustment.

metrics.name: Average Daily Trading Volume
metrics.calculation_notes: Average number of shares traded per day over the trailing 90-day period. Contextual liquidity measure only — excluded from correlation analysis. ADTV is a consequence of investor demand and index inclusion, not a fundamental characteristic management can influence.

metrics.name: Passive Ownership Percentage
metrics.calculation_notes: Shares held by passive/index funds / total shares outstanding * 100. Contextual ownership structure measure — excluded from correlation analysis. Passive ownership is a consequence of index inclusion decisions and market cap thresholds, not a fundamental driver.

metrics.name: Free Float Percentage
metrics.calculation_notes: (Total shares outstanding - insider/founder/strategic holdings) / total shares outstanding * 100. Contextual measure of trading availability — excluded from correlation analysis. Free float is a structural artifact of founding/ownership history, not a management-actionable lever.

correlation_design.rationale: Non-parametric, robust to outliers and non-normality, appropriate for small N (~25). Does not require distributional assumptions.
correlation_design.significance_note: With N~25, Spearman rho of ~0.40 is statistically significant at p<0.05 (two-tailed). Report both rho and p-value for each test.
correlation_design.stable_driver_criterion: rho > 0.5 across all three multiples with p < 0.05
correlation_design.multiple_specific_criterion: rho > 0.5 for exactly one multiple with p < 0.05
correlation_design.moderate_signal_criterion: 0.3 <= rho <= 0.5 for at least one multiple
